addons.releaseNotes: v1.3.4: Sidebar icon now uses an inline lucide Sparkles SVG (h-5 w-5, stroke 2) so it visually matches the rest of the menu — was rendering as a small emoji 📄 before. Bundles v1.3.3's smaller chunks + 90s timeout, v1.3.2's model-ID fixes, and v1.3.1's pdfjs-dist 4.x downgrade.

addons.name: TR PDF Converter
addons.description: Native Trade Republic PDF parser — extracts cash, MMF, trading P&L, crypto (BTC/ETH/XRP/SOL with TR's pseudo-ISINs). Direct one-click import to Donkeyfolio activities (pick target account or auto-create TR account, extracts quantity + unit price + €1 fee for manual trades, €0 for savings plans). Crypto staking rewards classified as INTEREST + STAKING_REWARD subtype. Cash flows emit qty=1, unitPrice=amount per Donkeyfolio wizard convention. Supports DE/EN/PT TR statements. Format-aware number parsing (US & EU). Auto-recovery for broken rows. Ported from jcmpagel's open-source code.
addons.author: Donkeyfolio
addons.releaseNotes: v2.6.7: Ticker map corrections after user verification. Fixes 5 wrong/uncertain mappings shipped in v2.6.6: US0080731088 → AVAV (AeroVironment, was wrongly ABNB), US25402D1028 → DOCN (DigitalOcean Holdings, was wrongly DJT), US83443Q1031 → SOLST (Solstice Advanced Materials, was wrongly SOLM which is an Amplify Solana ETF), US5949724083 → MSTR (Strategy, formerly MicroStrategy), CA50077N1024 → PNG.TO (Kraken Robotics on TSX, was PNG.V which Yahoo resolved to a different security). All other mappings unchanged. v2.6.6: ISIN→ticker mapping (logos + live prices). v2.6.5 imported 3892/3971 trades but stored ISINs as canonical symbols, so Yahoo Finance couldn't sync prices for ~86 assets and the ticker-logos folder (indexed by ticker) showed no logos. v2.6.6 ships a curated ISIN→ticker map covering 130+ common TR holdings: US/CA equities map to plain Yahoo tickers (AAPL, MSFT, AMD, PLTR, NVDA…), Irish/LU ETFs map to LSE/Xetra/Paris listings (CSPX.L, EUNL.DE, VHYL.L, AEEM.PA…), and crypto pseudo-ISINs map to Yahoo crypto pairs (BTC-EUR, ETH-EUR, SOL-EUR, ADA-EUR, XRP-EUR). Unmapped ISINs fall back to the previous behavior (cost basis preserved, no live price). v2.6.5: Hotfix on top of v2.6.4 — defensive error coercion in the import catch block. Tauri IPC errors come back as plain strings (not Error objects), so (err as Error).message was undefined and msg.slice() crashed the whole loop with 'undefined is not an object (evaluating msg.slice)' on the very first failure, hiding the real backend error. v2.6.5 coerces strings/objects/anything else to a usable string and surfaces the actual error in the failureExamples panel. v2.6.4: CRITICAL FIX — quoteCcy + instrumentType. Earlier v2.6.x calls activities.create() per activity, but the SDK's TypeScript SymbolInput omits the quoteCcy and instrumentType fields that the Rust backend's validate_persisted_symbol_metadata REQUIRES for new EQUITY assets. Without these, every BUY/SELL/DIVIDEND for a new asset was rejected with 'Quote currency is required' — leading to 0 imported / 3971 skipped on a typical TR yearly statement. v2.6.4 sends quoteCcy='EUR' + instrumentType='EQUITY' for all stock/ETF ISINs, and instrumentType='CRYPTO' + quoteMode='MANUAL' for TR's pseudo-ISINs (XF000…). Pure cash flows (DEPOSIT/WITHDRAWAL with no symbol) skip the symbol payload entirely. The UI now also shows up to 3 sample failure messages when skipped > 0 so users can diagnose remaining issues. v2.6.3: Account picker (lets you pick existing account vs auto-create). v2.6.2: Switch to single-create() per activity (bulk import discards symbols).
